name: safe-fs
purpose: Safe filesystem operation wrappers: symlink guards, atomic write, advisory locking, BOM stripping, and central MASTER_DEBT dual-write helpers.
notes: 757 lines. CJS (require()). Exports: isSafeToWrite, safeWriteFileSync, safeAppendFileSync, safeRenameSync, safeAtomicWriteSync, readUtf8Sync, readTextWithSizeGuard, streamLinesSync, acquireLock, releaseLock, withLock, writeMasterDebtSync, appendMasterDebtSync. Two-level require() fallback for symlink-guard path (scripts/lib vs .claude/skills/<skill>/scripts/lib). MASTER_DEBT section hardcodes docs/technical-debt/ path — sanitize_fields apply. Has 8 copies in skill subdirs (has_copies_at field below). JASON-OS counterpart exists but DIVERGED: SoNash=757L, JASON-OS=636L. SoNash is newer/larger — MASTER_DEBT section likely added after JASON-OS port.
jason_os_status: diverged — JASON-OS is 121 lines shorter, missing MASTER_DEBT writers

name: sanitize-error.cjs
purpose: CJS wrapper exposing sanitizeError, sanitizeErrorForJson, createSafeLogger, safeErrorMessage — scrubs home paths, credentials, connection strings, and private IPs from error messages.
notes: 97 lines. CJS. Implements the same SENSITIVE_PATTERNS as sanitize-error.js (ESM twin). Must stay in sync with sanitize-error.js manually — no shared source. JASON-OS counterpart exists but DIVERGED: header comment differs (JASON-OS says 'canonical CJS implementation' vs SoNash says 'CJS wrapper for sanitize-error.js (ESM)'). SoNash has 97 diff-lines; JASON-OS has 65 diff-lines — substantive divergence. JASON-OS version is actually longer (98L vs 97L per wc) — slight difference. Both are CJS with same exports.
jason_os_status: diverged — header and possibly minor logic differ; same exports

name: sanitize-error.d.ts
purpose: TypeScript type declarations for sanitize-error.js — defines SanitizeOptions, SafeLogger interfaces, and exports for sanitizeError, sanitizeErrorForJson, createSafeLogger, safeErrorMessage.
notes: 28 lines. Type declaration file (.d.ts). Created to avoid tsx circular resolution (tsx resolves .js imports to .ts files). JASON-OS counterpart: IDENTICAL (0 diff lines). Note: .d.ts is not .cjs or .esm — module_system field is not applicable, use 'none'.
jason_os_status: identical

name: sanitize-error.js
purpose: ESM canonical implementation of error sanitization — same SENSITIVE_PATTERNS as .cjs twin but exported as named ESM exports.
notes: 149 lines. ESM (export function). Must stay in sync with sanitize-error.cjs manually. The .cjs and .js twins implement identical logic; the split exists because hooks/scripts use require() while some callers use import(). The .d.ts file provides types for the .js ESM version. NO JASON-OS counterpart — this ESM variant is not ported. Only the .cjs and .d.ts are in JASON-OS scripts/lib/. JASON-OS gap: if any JASON-OS ESM scripts need sanitizeError they must either use .cjs or add this file.
jason_os_status: missing — ESM twin not ported; only .cjs and .d.ts ported

name: security-helpers
purpose: Security primitives library: sanitizeDisplayString, escapeMd, refuseSymlinkWithParents, validatePathInDir, safeWriteFile, safeGitAdd, safeGitCommit, sanitizeFilename, parseCliArgs, safeReadFile, validateUrl, safeRegexExec, maskEmail, slugify.
notes: 506 lines. CJS. Re-exports sanitizeError from sanitize-error.cjs and isSafeToWrite from symlink-guard. Wide-coverage helper — covers string sanitization, path traversal prevention, git operations, CLI arg parsing, URL allowlist (SSRF), regex safety, PII masking, and slugification. JASON-OS counterpart exists but DIVERGED: SoNash=506L, JASON-OS=558L. JASON-OS is 52 lines longer — JASON-OS appears to have additions beyond SoNash version. Full structural diff not read, but the files differ substantially.
jason_os_status: diverged — JASON-OS is 52 lines longer, has additions. Direction of drift unclear without full diff.

name: parse-jsonl-line
purpose: Two JSONL line parsers: safeParseLine (returns object|null silently) and safeParseLineWithError (returns {value, error} for callers that need to report bad-line positions).
notes: 69 lines. CJS (use strict + module.exports). Created specifically to satisfy T39 pattern-compliance detector: bare JSON.parse in loops without exact try/catch shape triggers a lint rule; this lib provides conforming wrappers. Also copied into 3+ skill subdirs (doc-ecosystem-audit, health-ecosystem-audit at minimum). JASON-OS counterpart: IDENTICAL (0 diff lines). Pure utility — no SoNash-specific logic.
jason_os_status: identical

name: read-jsonl
purpose: Read a JSONL file and return parsed items array, with options for safe mode (return [] on error) and quiet mode (suppress per-line warnings).
notes: 54 lines. CJS. Simple JSONL reader with two modes: safe=true returns [] on file error (good for optional files), quiet=true suppresses malformed-line warnings. JASON-OS counterpart: files are same length (54L each) BUT differ by 110 diff-lines (full replacement — same interface, different implementation or formatting). Functionally equivalent but not byte-identical. Pure utility.
jason_os_status: diverged — same line count but differ substantially; likely interface-compatible but different implementation

name: todos-mutations
purpose: Pure functions for /todo skill JSONL mutations: validate, add, edit, complete, progress, delete, reprioritize, archive, with regression guard to prevent silent data loss.
notes: 391 lines. CJS. Pure mutation library — no file I/O, no process.exit (extracted from todos-cli.js for testability). Created to fix T26/T27/T28 data-loss bug where compaction context caused stale overwrites. Core of the /todo skill persistence safety. JASON-OS counterpart: IDENTICAL (0 diff lines, same 391L). Fully ported and in sync.
jason_os_status: identical

name: safe-cas-io

notes: 249 lines. CJS. Created in PR #505 to address Qodo parent-chain symlink gaps in the CAS script family. Uses O_NOFOLLOW (POSIX) with graceful Windows degradation.
